description.en: Understand StartMessaging API rate limits, throughput limits, and best practices for high-volume messaging.

title.pt: Limites de API e Rate Limiting
title.es: Límites de API y Rate Limiting

subtitle.en: Understand throughput limits, rate limiting policies, and best practices for handling high-volume messaging with StartMessaging's API.
subtitle.pt: Entenda limites de throughput, políticas de rate limiting e melhores práticas para mensagens de alto volume.
subtitle.es: Comprenda los límites de rendimiento, las políticas de limitación de tasa y las mejores prácticas para la mensajería de alto volumen.
subtitle.id: Pahami batas throughput, kebijakan rate limiting, dan praktik terbaik untuk menangani pesan volume tinggi.
subtitle.ar: فهم حدود السرعة وسياسات التحكم في المعدل وأفضل الممارسات للمراسلة عالية الحجم.
subtitle.tr: Verim sınırlarını, hız sınırlama politikalarını ve yüksek hacimli mesajlaşma için en iyi uygulamaları anlayın.

body.en:
Free Plan

  • 10 API requests per second
  • 1,000 messages per month
  • 100 contacts per broadcast

Premium Plan

  • 100 API requests per second
  • 10,000 free messages per month (then pay-as-you-go)
  • 10,000 contacts per broadcast
  • Webhook delivery: real-time

Enterprise Plan

  • Custom rate limits (up to 1,000 req/s)
  • Unlimited messages (volume pricing)
  • 100,000+ contacts per broadcast
  • Dedicated infrastructure
  • Custom SLA

WhatsApp Throughput (Set by Meta)

WhatsApp message throughput is controlled by Meta based on your phone number's quality rating and messaging tier:

  • Tier 1: 1,000 unique users/24h
  • Tier 2: 10,000 unique users/24h
  • Tier 3: 100,000 unique users/24h
  • Tier 4 (Unlimited): Unlimited unique users/24h

New numbers start at Tier 1 and automatically upgrade as you send messages and maintain a good quality rating. Our system handles all throttling automatically.

Best Practices

  • Use exponential backoff when you receive 429 (rate limit) responses
  • Batch contacts in broadcast calls instead of individual sends
  • Use webhooks instead of polling for message status
  • Contact us to increase limits for your account

question.en: What happens when I hit a rate limit?

answer.en: You receive a 429 (Too Many Requests) HTTP response with a Retry-After header indicating when you can retry. We recommend implementing exponential backoff in your integration. For broadcast operations, our engine handles rate limiting automatically — you don't need to manage Meta's throughput limits yourself.

question.en: Can I increase my rate limits?

answer.en: Yes. Premium plans have higher default limits (100 req/s), and Enterprise plans offer custom rate limits up to 1,000 req/s. Contact our sales team to discuss your volume requirements.

subtext.en: Enterprise plans offer custom rate limits and dedicated infrastructure.
